allies.rationale_text: Game-theory-payoffs helps calculated-risk-taking reason through adversarial choices, countermoves, and payoff structure before committing.
Decision-trees gives calculated-risk-taking a concrete branching structure for comparing uncertain paths and explicit payoffs.

antagonists.rationale_text: Optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy undermines calculated-risk-taking by overweighting upside scenarios and underestimating the real downside exposure.
antagonists.source_quote: focusing too heavily on the mean outcome

structured_tensions.tension_text: Calculated-risk-taking conflicts with confirmation-bias when risk estimates are calibrated to justify a preferred course of action rather than derived from independent evidence.
structured_tensions.source_quote: calculated-risk-taking conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when risk estimates are calibrated to justify a preferred course of action rather than derived from independent evidence.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Calculated-risk-taking conflicts with black-swan-events when systematic risk estimation creates false precision around outcomes whose probability distributions have fat tails.
structured_tensions.source_quote: calculated-risk-taking conflicts with black-swan-events (Black Swan events) when systematic risk estimation creates false precision around outcomes whose probability distributions have fat tails.

curation_notes.summary: The file keeps the strongest first-order relation surface: explicit branch-comparison allies and a clear antagonist in upside-biased risk sizing.
donor_drops: Did not manufacture structured tensions because the raw source is more ally-and-anti-pattern heavy than explicit tension-block based.
open_questions: Reference classes are source-real here too, but decision-trees and game-theory-payoffs were the more operational anchors.
